question: [File: 'File Name: rest/tokens.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getTokenBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /api/v1/tokens/:tokenId/balances?account.balance=gte:0 (no timestamp filter) to trigger the non-timestamp path where `balanceConditions` are pushed directly into the main `conditions` array (line 669-671), causing the `filtered_token_accounts` CTE to filter by balance after scanning all associated token accounts, maximizing the scan range?

[File: 'File Name: rest/tokens.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: extractSqlFromNftTokenInfoRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user send rapid concurrent GET /api/v1/tokens/:tokenId/nfts/:serialNumber requests for a valid token/serial pair to cause repeated DB queries against the `nft` table with a LEFT JOIN on `entity`, and since there is no caching for individual NFT info, exhaust DB connection pool slots under high concurrency?

[File: 'File Name: rest/tokens.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getTokensRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /api/v1/tokens?name=abc&order=desc with the name filter to trigger an ILIKE scan that cannot use the token_id ORDER BY index (since results are ordered by `t.token_id` but filtered by name), forcing a full table scan followed by an in-memory sort, compounding CPU and memory usage?

[File: 'File Name: rest/tokens.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getTokensRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `hasNameParam` disables pagination links (line 405-406) by sending repeated GET /api/v1/tokens?name=X requests, knowing that each request performs a full ILIKE scan with no cursor-based optimization, making every request equally expensive regardless of prior requests?

[File: 'File Name: rest/tokens.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getTokensRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /api/v1/tokens?token.id=eq:1&token.id=eq:2&...&token.id=eq:N&name=abc and observe that the mutual exclusion check (line 362-364) throws an error, but by removing the name param and keeping only many token.id=eq: values, force an unbounded `= any($N)` array parameter that causes the DB to perform an expensive ANY scan?

[File: 'File Name: rest/tokens.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: extractSqlFromTokenBalancesRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /api/v1/tokens/:tokenId/balances?account.id=gt:0.0.1&account.id=lt:0.0.999999999 with multiple account.id range filters to accumulate multiple conditions in the `conditions` array, generating a compound WHERE clause that forces the DB to evaluate multiple range predicates on `ti.account_id` simultaneously?

[File: 'File Name: rest/tokens.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getNftTransferHistoryRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /api/v1/tokens/:tokenId/nfts/:serialNumber/transactions?timestamp=gt:0&timestamp=lt:9999999999 with wide timestamp range filters to force the UNION ALL query in `extractSqlFromNftTransferHistoryRequest()` to scan the entire `nft_history` table for the given token/serial, returning the maximum number of timestamps and then triggering an expensive `= any($4)` scan on the transaction table?

[File: 'File Name: rest/tokens.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getNftTransferHistoryRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent GET /api/v1/tokens/:tokenId/nfts/:serialNumber/transactions requests for a high-activity NFT (many transfers) to trigger the two-phase query pattern — timestamp fetch then detail fetch — consuming two DB connections per request and exhausting the connection pool at half the concurrency level compared to single-query endpoints?

[File: 'File Name: rest/tokens.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getNftTransferHistoryRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `NftService.getNft()` call at line 1047 which performs a third DB query after the two-phase query pattern, meaning each request to `/tokens/:tokenId/nfts/:serialNumber/transactions` consumes up to three DB connections sequentially, tripling the connection pool pressure under concurrent load?

[File: 'File Name: rest/tokens.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getTokenInfoRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent GET /api/v1/tokens/:tokenId requests without a timestamp filter to trigger `TokenService.putTokenCache(token)` on every response, and by targeting many different valid token IDs cause cache thrashing that evicts useful entries and forces repeated DB queries for previously cached tokens?

[File: 'File Name: rest/tokens.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getTokenBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /api/v1/tokens/:tokenId/balances with a valid timestamp filter to trigger `TokenService.getCachedTokens()` (line 726) after the main query, adding a cache lookup overhead to every balance request, and by targeting tokens not in cache force additional DB queries for token decimals on every balance response?

[File: 'File Name: rest/tokens.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: extractSqlFromTokenRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /api/v1/tokens?token.id=gt:0&token.id=lt:9999999999999 with range filters on token.id to generate a compound WHERE clause on `t.token_id` that, combined with the ORDER BY `t.token_id` and LIMIT, forces the DB to scan a large portion of the token table before applying the limit, causing high I/O?

[File: 'File Name: rest/tokens.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getTokensRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /api/v1/tokens?account.id=0.0.X&publickey=<hex> to trigger both the `tokenAccountCte` path (adding a CTE over token_account) and the `publickey` filter (adding a condition on `e.public_key`), combining two expensive operations in a single query and causing compounded DB load?

[File: 'File Name: rest/tokens.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: extractSqlFromNftTokensRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /api/v1/tokens/:tokenId/nfts?account.id=0.0.X&serialnumber=gt:1&serialnumber=lt:9999999999 with multiple filter combinations to generate a multi-condition WHERE clause on the `nft` table with a LEFT JOIN on `entity`, and by targeting a token with millions of NFTs cause full-range scans that exhaust DB I/O?

[File: 'File Name: rest/tokens.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getTokenInfoRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /api/v1/tokens/0x<valid_evm_address>?timestamp=lte:X to trigger EVM address resolution via `getAndValidateTokenIdRequestPathParam()` followed by the full `buildHistoryQuery()` UNION ALL pattern, adding an extra address resolution step before the expensive history query and compounding the per-request cost?

[File: 'File Name: rest/tokens.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getNftTokenInfoRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user send rapid concurrent GET /api/v1/tokens/:tokenId/nfts/:serialNumber requests for non-existent serial numbers to force the DB to execute the full `nft` + `entity` LEFT JOIN query and return zero rows, triggering a `NotFoundError` on every request, and by flooding with such requests exhaust DB connections without any caching benefit?

[File: 'File Name: rest/tokens.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: extractSqlFromTokenBalancesRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /api/v1/tokens/:tokenId/balances?timestamp=eq:X (which gets transformed to `lte` by `transformTimestampFilterOp()`) to trigger `balances.getAccountBalanceTimestampRange()` which itself runs a DB query against `account_balance` for the treasury account, meaning every token balance request with a timestamp filter costs at minimum two DB queries?

[File: 'File Name: rest/tokens.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getTokensRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /api/v1/tokens?name=X&order=asc with a name filter to trigger an ILIKE scan that returns results ordered by `t.token_id ASC` (line 209), forcing the DB to scan all matching rows and sort them by token_id rather than using an index, causing high memory usage for large result sets?

[File: 'File Name: rest/tokens.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: extractSqlFromTokenRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /api/v1/tokens?type=FUNGIBLE_COMMON&publickey=<hex>&token.id=gt:0 with multiple filter types simultaneously to generate a complex WHERE clause combining type, public_key, and token_id conditions on a three-table join (token + entity + optional token_account), causing the query planner to choose a suboptimal plan?
